risks.mitigation: Maintain 12-16h growlight photoperiod, hold steady moisture and EC, pinch out flower spikes on sight, and harvest tips on a strict 7-14 day cycle to keep the vine vegetative.

growth_stages.description: Start from scarified pre-soaked seed in plugs, or prepare 20-25cm tip cuttings - the faster, preferred route in the tropics.
actions: For seed: sandpaper-scarify and soak 24h, sow 5-6mm deep in rockwool at pH 5.8, hold 24-30C
For cuttings: strip lower leaves, callus in a dark damp space 1-2 days
Keep media moist with plain water or 0.6-0.8 mS/cm at most, no strong nutrients yet

growth_stages.description: Seeds germinate slowly (10-21 days); cuttings push roots in 5-10 days. Establish the root system before feeding hard.
actions: Maintain 24-30C and 70-85% humidity over the propagation tray
Give gentle 150-200 PPFD light or bright shade; move rooted cuttings to net pots once roots are 3-5cm

growth_stages.description: Young plants set 2-4 true leaf pairs and begin vining; transplant into the main DWC/NFT/Kratky system.
actions: Raise EC to ~1.0-1.2 mS/cm, keep pH 5.8-6.3
Set up a light trellis/net and begin training the lead shoot upward

growth_stages.description: Rapid vine elongation and leaf expansion; thick succulent leaves form as the plant climbs its support.
actions: Raise EC to 1.6 mS/cm, hold pH 6.0-6.3
Provide 8-12h sun or 12-16h LED at 250-400 PPFD, 30% afternoon shade in peak heat
Keep reservoir 22-28C, DO above 5 mg/L; tie/clip vines and pinch the tip once to force laterals

growth_stages.description: Vines reach 30-45cm with several thick glossy leaf pairs; take the first cut-and-come-again tip harvest.
actions: Cut 15-20cm tender tips with 4-6 leaves just above a node in early morning
Cool harvested shoots to 10-12C within 1 hour; sanitize cutting blades between plants

growth_stages.description: Each cut forces new laterals; the vine yields tender shoots repeatedly, with cumulative per-plant yield climbing over months.
actions: Re-cut 15-20cm shoots every 7-14 days above a node
Maintain EC 2.0-2.4 mS/cm (cap at 2.4 to limit nitrate), pH 6.0-6.5
Scout weekly for aphids/leaf spot; pinch any flower spikes immediately to keep growth vegetative

growth_stages.description: A well-kept vine produces for 3-6 months; growth eventually slows and stems lignify, signaling replacement from fresh cuttings.
actions: Continue harvest cycle while shoots stay tender; do a low-EC flush 2-3 days before large cuts
Take fresh 20-25cm cuttings to start the next succession crop
Retire the plant once stems toughen or disease pressure rises, sanitize and restart

common_issues: Fusarium wilt (Fusarium oxysporum f.sp. niveum) in recirculating systems; gummy stem blight and anthracnose in humid canopies; powdery mildew on upper leaf surface and downy mildew on lower surface during PH wet season; blossom-end rot of fruit from Ca deficiency or EC/irrigation swings; aphids and whiteflies vectoring mosaic viruses; spider mites in hot dry rooms; melon/fruit flies (Bactrocera) puncturing developing fruit; poor fruit set and aborted fruitlets from inadequate pollination indoors; hollow heart and cracking from erratic EC/water during fill; pythium root rot when water temp >26C lowers dissolved oxygen; magnesium and potassium drawdown during heavy fruit fill
harvest_indicator: Tendril nearest the fruit stem dries brown; ground/field spot turns from white to creamy-yellow; skin loses gloss and becomes dull/waxy; dull hollow thump when tapped (not a high ring); icebox fruit 1.5-3.5kg and full-size 4-8kg; flesh 9-12 Brix; 35-45 days after pollination
source: HydroponicsUp watermelon guide, Lanitex AU melon guide, NIH PMC12271344 GH watermelon EC/Brix study, UF-IFAS, Clemson HGIC, Bayer/Rupp triploid pollenizer guides, PH prices (Tridge/Selina Wamucii)
tips: Use Dutch buckets with 15-20L of 60% coco coir / 40% perlite per plant, one vine per bucket, on a recirculating drip line; a 5-10 gal bucket gives the heavy root system room.
Pick compact icebox/personal-size cultivars (Sugar Baby, Mini Love, Yellow Doll) for vertical hydro so each 1.5-3.5kg fruit stays manageable on the trellis; full-size types overload slings.
Train one main vine up a STRONG vertical trellis (rebar/cattle-panel grade); pinch lateral runners and tendrils weekly to a single leader and keep the canopy open for airflow.
Ramp EC in stages: seedling 1.2-1.5, vegetative 2.0-2.5, raise to 2.8-3.2 mS/cm at fruit set to drive sugar loading; hold pH 5.8-6.2 throughout.

Hand-pollinate every morning 6-10am: pick a freshly open male flower, strip the petals, and dab the loaded anther onto the stigma of each open female (recognizable by the swollen ovary behind the flower); one male covers 2-3 females.
For seedless (triploid) cultivars you MUST interplant a diploid pollenizer (every 5th-6th plant) or hand-carry diploid pollen — triploids set no fruit without viable diploid pollen.
Sling every retained fruit once it reaches tennis-ball size: support it in a soft mesh/fabric net (onion bag works) tied independently to the trellis frame so a 2-5kg melon does not tear the vine.
Limit load to 2-4 fruits per icebox vine (1-2 for larger types); remove the first 1-2 low female flowers and thin excess fruitlets so the keepers size and sweeten.
Keep root-zone/reservoir water below 24C; above 26C dissolved oxygen falls and Pythium risk rises — add an air stone or shade the reservoir in PH lowland heat.
Provide 14-16h of strong full-spectrum light at canopy 500-700 PPFD indoors; outdoors target 8+ hours direct sun for adequate fruit sugars.
Hold a steady Ca supply (CalMag ~0.5-1.0 g/L) and avoid EC/irrigation swings during fruit fill to prevent blossom-end rot; never let buckets dry then flood.
In the last ~10-14 days before ripeness apply a mild controlled water/EC reduction (slightly lower irrigation frequency, hold EC near top of range) to concentrate sugars and lift Brix to 10-12.
Net or screen the house against melon/fruit flies (Bactrocera) and bag individual fruit; run yellow sticky traps for aphids/whiteflies that vector mosaic viruses.
Confirm ripeness with multiple signs together: dried fruit-stem tendril, creamy-yellow ground spot, dull skin, and a low hollow thump — then cut, do not pull, the fruit.

risks.title: Poor fruit set / failed pollination
risks.description: Watermelon is monoecious and insect-pollinated; in screened or indoor hydro setups there are no bees, so female flowers abort and fruitlets yellow and drop. Seedless triploids fail entirely without viable diploid pollen.
risks.mitigation: Hand-pollinate every open female 6-10am with fresh male anthers; for triploids interplant a diploid pollenizer every 5th-6th plant or hand-carry diploid pollen; keep day temps 24-30C for pollen viability.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot and fruit cracking
risks.description: Localized Ca deficiency at the blossom end, driven by interrupted irrigation or swinging EC, causes a sunken leathery rot; sudden water/EC surges during fill split fruit and cause hollow heart.
risks.mitigation: Maintain steady Ca (CalMag 0.5-1.0 g/L), hold EC stable in range, irrigate little-and-often so buckets never dry out, and make any ripening water cut-back gradual not abrupt.

risks.title: Fusarium wilt and Pythium root rot
risks.description: Fusarium oxysporum f.sp. niveum spreads through recirculating solution, wilting whole vines from one side; warm water (>26C) and low oxygen invite Pythium that browns and rots roots.
risks.mitigation: Use Fusarium-resistant or grafted rootstock, sanitize buckets/lines between crops, keep water 20-24C with aeration, and isolate/cull infected plants; do not reuse media from a wilted crop.

risks.title: Foliar disease: powdery/downy mildew, gummy stem blight, anthracnose
risks.description: Dense humid canopies in PH wet-season conditions invite powdery mildew (white upper-leaf patches), downy mildew (lower-leaf yellowing), and gummy stem blight/anthracnose on stems and fruit.
risks.mitigation: Train to a single open leader, keep humidity 60-70% with active airflow, avoid wetting foliage, scout twice weekly, and rotate protectant fungicides (e.g. sulfur/potassium bicarbonate, copper) on label rates.

risks.title: Sucking pests and fruit flies
risks.description: Aphids, whiteflies and spider mites colonize the canopy and vector mosaic viruses; melon/fruit flies (Bactrocera) sting developing fruit causing internal rot and maggots.
risks.mitigation: Screen the structure, run yellow sticky traps, release/encourage predators or apply insecticidal soap/neem; bag individual fruit and deploy methyl-eugenol fruit-fly traps around the house.

risks.title: Vine/fruit collapse from inadequate support
risks.description: A maturing 2-5kg fruit hanging on a vertical vine will tear the stem or pull the plant off the trellis, destroying the fruit and damaging the vine.
risks.mitigation: Build a heavy-duty trellis and place each fruit in a soft mesh sling tied directly to the frame once it reaches tennis-ball size; limit fruit load to 2-4 per icebox vine.

risks.title: Low Brix / bland fruit
risks.description: Over-watering, excess nitrogen late, or too many fruits per vine dilute sugars, giving watery low-Brix melons that fetch poor prices.
risks.mitigation: Thin to 2-4 fruits, hold EC 2.8-3.2 at fruiting, taper nitrogen and apply a mild water/EC reduction in the final 10-14 days, and harvest only when ripeness signs confirm 9-12 Brix.

growth_stages.description: Sow 1 seed per rockwool/coco plug 1-2cm deep; watermelon needs warmth to germinate.
actions: Hold 28-30C day, no nutrients or very dilute EC <=0.8
Keep humid until cotyledons emerge in 5-7 days

growth_stages.description: True leaves form; build a strong root system before transplant into buckets.
actions: Transplant to Dutch bucket at 2-3 true leaves

growth_stages.description: Vine extends rapidly and climbs; establish the single-leader trellis structure.
actions: Train main vine up strong trellis, pinch lateral runners

growth_stages.description: Male flowers open first, then females with a swollen ovary; pollination window is critical.
actions: Hand-pollinate females 6-10am with fresh male anthers
For seedless types ensure diploid pollenizer pollen is present
Maintain EC ~2.5, day temps 24-30C for pollen viability

growth_stages.description: Successful fruitlets swell past golf-ball size; select and thin the keepers.
actions: Thin to 2-4 fruits per icebox vine
Begin sling support once fruit reaches tennis-ball size, tied to trellis frame
Hold steady Ca (CalMag 0.5-1.0 g/L) to prevent blossom-end rot

growth_stages.description: Fruit bulks up; sugar loading begins as flesh thickens and color deepens.
actions: Scout for mildew, aphids and fruit flies; bag individual fruit

growth_stages.description: Final sugar concentration; controlled mild water/EC reduction sharpens Brix.
actions: Cut irrigation frequency slightly the last 10-14 days, hold EC near top of range
Watch the fruit-stem tendril dry and ground spot turn creamy-yellow
Stop nitrogen pushes; let sugars peak

growth_stages.description: Fruit reaches full Brix and size; multiple ripeness signs confirm readiness 35-45 days after pollination.
actions: Confirm dried tendril, creamy-yellow ground spot, dull skin and low hollow thump
Cut (do not pull) the stem with a sanitized blade
Cool promptly; icebox fruit ~1.5-3.5kg at 9-12 Brix
